//: Tailscale ACL Configuration — Wheeler AIOps Infrastructure
Security Rationale:
Tailscale ACLs (Access Control Lists) govern which machines in
the Tailscale mesh can communicate with each other, on which
ports, and by which users. They are the AUTHORITATIVE policy
layer for ALL tailnet traffic.
Unlike UFW (which runs on each machine individually), Tailscale
ACLs are CENTRALLY MANAGED in the Tailscale admin console or
checked into your repo. This ensures consistent policy even if
a server's local firewall is misconfigured.
These ACLs enforce:
1. Admin users have full access to all machines for management
2. Hostinger (edge) can only reach specific Hetzner ports for
3. Hetzner can reach Hostinger's database ports for sync
4. Auto-approvers allow automatic subnet route propagation
Tag Definitions:
tag:hetzner     — Primary AIOps orchestrator server
tag:hostinger   — Public edge / frontend server
tag:admin       — Administrator workstations with full access
Deployment:
Apply via Tailscale admin console (ACLs tab), or
via tailscale CLI: tailscale set --acls-file=tailscale-acls.json
Documentation:
https://tailscale.com/kb/1018/acls/

autoApprovers.//: Auto-approve subnet routes from tagged devices
Hetzner can advertise routes to Docker networks (172.x.x.x)
so admin machines can reach containers directly.

acls.//: Admin users can access ALL ports on ALL tagged servers.
This is necessary for SSH, debugging, and management.
Since admin machines are personal workstations (not public
servers), this is a reasonable trust model.
Hostinger's Traefik reverse proxies traffic to Hetzner's
backend services. Hostinger ONLY needs access to the
specific ports that serve web traffic.
Hetzner needs to reach Hostinger's databases for
Hetzner can access all of its own services.
Hostinger can access all of its own services.
Essential for Docker health checks and local monitoring.
Ping is useful for connectivity testing and monitoring.

//: SSH: Allow Tailscale SSH for admin convenience
Tailscale SSH provides an additional SSH access path that
bypasses the server's sshd_config. This is useful for emergency
access if SSH is misconfigured.
Node attributes for additional security properties
This marks our servers so Tailscale can enforce additional checks.
